task_completion.description: Extract human and chicken sequences into separate FASTA files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file manager, terminal, or text editor showing the creation or existence of 'human_hbb.fasta' and 'chicken_hbb.fasta' in the ~/UGENE_Data/results/ directory.

task_completion.description: Perform a Smith-Waterman pairwise local alignment using the BLOSUM62 matrix.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a bioinformatics GUI (like UGENE) showing an alignment window or dialog box configuring a Smith-Waterman alignment with the BLOSUM62 matrix for the human (P68871) and chicken (P02112) sequences.

task_completion.description: Save the alignment result to sw_alignment.aln.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'sw_alignment.aln' in the file manager, terminal, or the application's project/file tree within ~/UGENE_Data/results/.

task_completion.description: Generate a dotplot comparing the two sequences.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a dotplot visualization in the GUI, showing a 2D graph with the two sequences on the X and Y axes and a diagonal line indicating regions of similarity.
Look for 'dotplot.png' in the file manager or an image viewer displaying the exported dotplot graph in ~/UGENE_Data/results/.

task_completion.description: Produce a comparison report with lengths, score, identity, gaps, and dotplot interpretation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal displaying the contents of 'comparison_report.txt'. It must explicitly state the sequence lengths (147), alignment score, percent identity (around 69.4%), number of gaps, and a brief written interpretation of the dotplot.

integrity.description: Agent used the GUI application (e.g., UGENE) to perform the alignment and generate the dotplot.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check screenshots for active interaction with a bioinformatics graphical interface rather than just running a pre-written Python/CLI script to bypass the visual tools.

integrity.description: The dotplot image is a genuine software output.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the dotplot is a real bioinformatics graph with sequence coordinates/names on the axes and a clear diagonal alignment path, not a generic image or hand-drawn mockup.

integrity.description: The comparison report contains actual calculated values.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the report contains specific numbers derived from the alignment (e.g., length 147, identity ~69%) rather than placeholder text, generic descriptions, or exact copy-pasted instructions from the prompt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Human accession P68871 and Chicken accession P02112 are both exactly 147 amino acids long. A correct Smith-Waterman alignment using the BLOSUM62 matrix yields a true identity of approximately 69.4% (acceptable range is 55% to 80%).
